title: Does the MAGI system has souls
display_title: Does the MAGI system has souls
wikitext:
[]: Does the MAGI system has souls?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Sun Jun 26, 2016 2:15 pm I'm wondering if the MAGI system, that apparently was set up with Dr. Akagi senior's neural engrams, has a soul or souls. Especially after Dr. Akagi senior's demise that possibility would open itself. I'm interested in your thoughts and speculations on that matter. Cheers Trax

[]: Re: Does the MAGI system has souls?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 26, 2016 3:45 pm Given that there are multiple MAGI installations across the various NERV locations, and in Tokyo-3 all three are running Naoko Akagi engrams, I suspect none of them are ensouled.

[]: Re: Does the MAGI system has souls?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 26, 2016 4:12 pm Why would the Magi system have a soul? The reason Evas have them is related to their specialized functions -- so that they can generate A.T. Fields to fight the Angels and, eventually, carry out HIP. A supercomputer's purpose is to, well, be a supercomputer, not engage the Angels directly in combat. If Gehirn/Nerv had planned for a contingency where the Magi system could fight off an Angelic invader with its own ATF, episode 13 would have played out much, much differently.
